seoTitle: How to Use Trello's Planner and Calendar Views
metaDescription: Learn how to turn a Trello board into a visual planner using Calendar and Timeline views, so deadlines and weekly agendas stay easy to track.
intro: Trello can act as a full visual planner once you start using its Calendar and Timeline views alongside a well-organized board. Instead of scattered tasks and missed milestones, you get a chronological view of everything due that day, week, or month. This guide walks through setting up planning lists, assigning due dates, and switching between calendar and timeline layouts to manage your schedule.

steps.heading: Set up your planning lists
steps.body: Log in to Trello and create lists for a weekly backlog plus one list per weekday, giving you dedicated lanes for organizing daily agenda items.

steps.heading: Add tasks and assign due dates
steps.body: Create cards for your agenda items inside the relevant day column, then open each card to assign team members, add labels, and set a specific due date.

steps.heading: Switch to Calendar view
steps.body: Use the workspace switcher to open Calendar view, where your cards appear slotted by due date, and toggle between a full month grid or a weekly agenda layout.

steps.heading: Use Timeline view for longer projects
steps.body: Open Timeline view to map multi-day projects side by side, then drag the edges of a task bar to extend or shorten its start and end dates.

steps.heading: Filter and confirm your schedule
steps.body: Filter the view by member or label to isolate specific commitments, then return to the standard board to confirm your date changes synced correctly.

faq.question: Do I need a Power-Up to use Trello's Calendar view?
faq.answer: Trello's Calendar and Timeline views are built into its native Workspace Views, so you can access them without installing a separate plugin.

faq.question: Can I drag tasks to reschedule them in Calendar view?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically drag cards to new dates directly within the calendar grid to adjust deadlines quickly.

faq.question: What's the difference between Calendar and Timeline view?
faq.answer: Calendar view slots cards by a single due date across a month or week, while Timeline view maps multi-day projects as horizontal bars you can stretch.

faq.question: Can I filter the planner by team member?
faq.answer: Yes, both views let you filter rows by member or label so you can isolate specific people's or categories' commitments.
